text:
Once upon an autumn morning, in the quaint forest nestled between two great mountains, a tiny red squirrel named Sandy was determined to retrieve the last nut for the winter feasting.

Sandy had learned tricks from the older squirrels; the nut often nested in a crooked oak tree whose branches seemed more myth than reality. Clutching a handful of acorns, Sandy scaled the tree with ease. The nut was there, nestled among the rustling leaves. It sparkled with the promise of summer sweetness.

Yet, a cunning chipmunk had already nibbled a tiny corner off the nut before Sandy could react. With a chirp of distress, Sandy scurried down the tree back to the forest floor, her acorn feeling strangely empty.

Disappointed, Sandy paused under a leafy brogue. She pondered over her next move. Her wise old feathered friend, Elder Willow, glided over. "A good finder will always look twice," Willow implored. "
